segments.text: Venice is technically impossible, but you still have massive stone places floating on a mud of swam. By all laws of physics, the city should have sunk.
Let's see how can you actually build buildings on this wet swam. For that,
they have to flip the entire forest upside down. They drove million of trees deep into the clay. The mud sealed the wood from oxygen and it did not rot.
Finally, it petrified into stone. But wait, you're in the ocean and you can't drink salt water. So for that they have turned the streets into a giant brea
filter. Every square is engineered to catch the rain, filter it through sand and store it underground. That's the city survival tank. But the real killer
is their seaweed system. There is no modern piping structure. But still where does it go? So they hacked the moon. They build tunnels that use the tides.
Twice a day the ocean rises, floods the system and naturally flushes the waste out to the sea. The study ven in detail
and you'll find so much interesting
